nodes.label: AI as efficient problem-solving tool
nodes.grounding_span: I enjoy the ease and effectiveness that I've gotten from AI in the past

nodes.label: AI as validation and perspective partner
nodes.grounding_span: the feeling of wanting to have a friend validate my idea

nodes.label: AI use for confidential or student-specific tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI use for confidential or student-specific tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Anything that is confidential or student-specific is always handled by me

nodes.label: AI for creative classroom materials
nodes.label_negative: AI-generated materials with errors or 'AI-like' appearance
nodes.grounding_span: I find that many AI models are not quite perfected in this

nodes.label: Student privacy and confidentiality
nodes.grounding_span: I do not ever send anything that includes personal information for my students

nodes.label: Authenticity and quality in teaching materials
nodes.grounding_span: there are often spelling mistakes or the images look too 'AI-like'

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as problem-solving partner
nodes.grounding_span: The most satisfied that I feel is usually when I've been stuck on a problem for a long time, and I finally feel like I have a solution thanks to AI

nodes.label: Frustration with AI factual errors
nodes.grounding_span: The only times when I've felt frustrated were when the AI gave me something that was factually incorrect

nodes.label: Private use of AI due to societal wariness
nodes.grounding_span: none of them know that I sometimes use it

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through feedback loops
nodes.grounding_span: I will let it know and have it make adjustments

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting for AI use
nodes.grounding_span: Anything that is confidential or student-specific is always handled by me
